steps.title: Find the defining relationship
steps.content: The sentence before the blank states the 424 BCE eclipse “was annular.” The sentence after the blank explains what annular means: “the Moon didn't cover the Sun completely, instead creating an annulus, or 'ring of fire.'” The blank must connect a term to its definition—a clarification, not a contrast, example, or parallel action.

steps.title: Match the transition to the logic
steps.content:
”That is” signals a clarification or restatement—exactly the relationship between the two sentences.

The other three options introduce different relationships: “Meanwhile” suggests simultaneous events, “For example” introduces a specific instance, and “Nonetheless” signals contrast.

This matches choice B — B is correct.
